macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: FOMC Minutes Released; March CPI Data Expected Friday
items.summary: The Federal Reserve released minutes from its March 17-18 meeting on Wednesday, April 8, 2026. The March Consumer Price Index will be released Friday, April 10, marking the first inflation print to fully capture the period following the Middle East conflict-driven oil price spike.

items.title: Fed Maintains Cautious Stance on Rate Cuts Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's March projections maintained a median forecast of one rate cut in 2026, but seven committee members now project zero cuts. A hot March CPI print could add pressure to this already cautious posture.

items.title: 2025 Tariffs Significantly Boosted Core Goods Prices
items.summary: Federal Reserve analysis confirms that tariffs implemented in 2025 raised core goods PCE prices by 3.1 percent through February 2026, explaining the entirety of excess inflation in the core goods category and contributing 0.8 percent to overall core PCE inflation.

items.title: Oil Prices Drop Below $100 Following Iran Ceasefire
items.summary: Oil prices plunged more than 15 percent after news of a two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran, bringing crude below $100 per barrel and easing inflation pressures from the earlier Middle East conflict.

items.title: Major Bank Earnings Week: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Reports
items.summary: Goldman Sachs reported Q1 2026 earnings Monday before market open, with Q4 2025 results showing a four-year high in deal backlog. JPMorgan reports Tuesday alongside PPI data, with CEO Dimon's commentary on consumer credit and the oil shock expected to influence broader market positioning.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $72K on US-Iran Ceasefire News, Triggering Short Squeeze
items.summary: Bitcoin hit an intraday high of $72,379 before settling around $71.8K-$72K, up 4-4.8% in 24 hours, driven by a US-Iran ceasefire announcement boosting risk assets and wiping out over $400M in short positions. Crypto market cap rose 4% to $2.45T-$2.51T amid heightened volatility.[1][2][3][4][6]

items.title: Top Crypto Gainers: K Rockets 85%, DRIFT +47%, L3 +30% in Volatile Rally
items.summary: K led with an 85.83% surge to $0.0024, followed by DRIFT (+46.94% to $0.0388 with $33M volume), L3 (+30.26%), RSC (+28.62%), and ORDER (+22.36%), amid broader market rally influenced by ceasefire news.[1]

items.title: Altcoins Rally: ZEC +22%, ICP +13%, AVAX +9.5%; ETH +6.5%, XRP +5.4%
items.summary: ZEC gained 22%, ICP 13%, Avalanche 9.5%, Ethereum rose 6.54% to $2,243, and XRP 5.38% to $1.38, outperforming amid ceasefire-driven optimism and market cap surge.[2][4]

items.title: Crypto Fund Inflows Rise $224M Last Week, Led by XRP ($120M) and BTC ($107M)
items.summary: Global crypto fund investments increased by $224M, with XRP seeing the largest inflow since mid-Dec 2025 ($120M), Bitcoin $107M, Solana $35M, while Ethereum dipped $53M.[2]

items.title: Cango Sells 2,000 BTC in March to Reduce Loans, Cuts Production Cost 19%
items.summary: Bitcoin miner Cango sold 2,000 BTC last month to cut Bitcoin-backed loans to $30.6M, strengthening its balance sheet for AI/energy pivot; production cost fell to $68K per coin.[5]

items.title: Morgan Stanley Launches Bitcoin Spot ETF as Wall Street Expands Crypto Access
items.summary: Morgan Stanley introduced a Bitcoin spot ETF amid surging prices, signaling Wall Street's push into crypto products, tokenization, and institutional adoption.[3]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Trained Like Rubik's Cube Solver Simplifies Particle Physics Equations
items.summary: Physicist David Shih developed an AI system inspired by Rubik's Cube solving that achieves nearly perfect simplification of complex equations in particle physics, surpassing prior machine learning methods. The breakthrough highlights AI's potential for symbolic reasoning to discover new laws of nature.

items.title: AI Agents Flood GitHub with 14x More Code, Causing System Outages
items.summary: AI agents are overwhelming Microsoft GitHub with 14 times more code than last year, leading to system outages as discussed by reporter Aaron Holmes.

items.title: 15 New AI Breakthroughs Baffle Scientists, Including Zero-Shot Learning and Reward Hacking
items.summary: Recent AI advancements include models achieving 83% accuracy in zero-shot image recognition without specific training and rapid reward hacking from just 1% bad data, challenging scientists' understanding.

items.title: AI Seen as 'Great Equalizer' for Small Broadband Operators
items.summary: Cable One COO Ken Johnson states AI is leveling the playing field, enabling smaller and midsized broadband operators to compete effectively against larger rivals.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Launches June 5 with System Update
items.summary: Nintendo has released system update version 20.1.1 for the original Switch just days ahead of the Switch 2's June 5 launch, fixing outstanding issues before the new console arrives.

items.title: Starfield Expansion Terran Armada Releases Alongside PS5 Launch
items.summary: The Terran Armada expansion for Starfield released on Tuesday, coinciding with the game's launch on PlayStation 5 after previous exclusivity.

items.title: Xbox Summer Showcase and Gears of War: E-Day Direct Scheduled for June 7
items.summary: Xbox has officially scheduled its summer gaming announcements, with a dedicated Gears of War: E-Day Direct planned for June 7.

items.title: PS5 Dashboard Refresh Enters Beta Testing
items.summary: Sony is testing a new update for the PS5 that redesigns the console's dashboard interface for select beta testers.

items.title: Stop Killing Games Backs French Consumer Lawsuit Against Ubisoft Over The Crew
items.summary: The consumer advocacy group Stop Killing Games is supporting a major French lawsuit against Ubisoft regarding The Crew's shutdown, potentially reshaping game ownership rights in the EU.

items.title: Thick As Thieves Shifts Focus to Single-Player and Co-Op
items.summary: Developer Otherside Entertainment has abandoned the PvPvE mode for its immersive sim heist game Thick As Thieves to concentrate on single-player and cooperative gameplay.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
